description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from large, irregularly shaped stone blocks, giving it a rugged and ancient appearance. The archway is supported by two thick, stone-clad pillars, each composed of stacked, roughly hewn stones that create a sense of mass and durability. The arch itself is a semi-circular arrangement of similar stones, forming a classic Romanesque curve. The surface of the stones is weathered and textured, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. There are no visible decorative reliefs, inscriptions, or ornate carvings, emphasizing a utilitarian and robust design. The overall color is a muted, earthy brown, consistent with natural stone.
description: A rugged, stone triumphal arch with thick, blocky pillars and a semi-circular archway, featuring a weathered, earthy brown surface and no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full archway and both stone pillars, highlighting the rough, blocky construction.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow depth of the arch and the stacked stone texture along the edge.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same archway and pillar structure visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and rugged stonework.
